name: Frozen Blueberry Cheesecake
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the crumbs, sugar, and butter in a small bowl and blend well.
2. Press firmly over the bottom of an 8-inch spring form pan.
3. Chill.
4. Combine the sugar, water, and cream of tartar in a small saucepan; bring to boiling.
5. Boil rapidly, 8 to 10 minutes, or until the syrup reaches 236 degrees on a candy thermometer (or until it forms soft peaks).
6. Meanwhile, in a large bowl of electric mixer beat egg whites until stiff peaks form.
7. Pour the hot syrup in a thin stream over the egg whites while beating constantly.
8. Continue beating until very stiff peaks form and the mixture cools, all together about 15 minutes.
9. Beat cream cheese and sour cream until light and fluffy; beat in vanilla and lemon rind.
10. Add 1/4 of the meringue to the cheese mixture, stirring to combine well.
11. Fold the remaining meringue into the cheese mixture until no streaks of meringue and cheese remain.
12. Spoon about 1/4 of the cheese mixture into the prepared pan; drizzle part of the blueberry preserves over it.
13. Continue to layer the cheese mixture and preserves, creating a marbled effect.
14. Freeze overnight or until firm.
15. Decorate with whipped cream and fresh or frozen unsweetened berries (Raspberries or strawberries may be substituted for blueberries).
